notes: The biggest cost risk is underpricing relative to local competitors. NG1 City Cleaners publishes from £50 for a single oven; Ovenu and Ovenclean average around £100 per order across multi-appliance jobs. A flat £60 price will attract volume but leaves little margin once fuel, chemicals, and insurance are stripped out — especially on a 2-hour single-oven job. Consider a tiered flat rate (single oven, double oven, oven + hob + extractor) rather than one flat price, which is how most established Nottingham operators actually work even when they call it 'bespoke'. The non-toxic positioning is a genuine differentiator worth charging for — the market evidence suggests customers will pay £80–£110 for a full kitchen clean. All figures here are AI estimates; get real quotes on insurance, chemical suppliers, and equipment before spending anything.

ongoing_costs.note: Rough estimate based on 8–12 jobs/month at part-time pace. Will scale directly with job volume.
Annual premium of roughly £100–£150 spread monthly. Confirm with a live quote — business activity description matters.

ongoing_costs.item: Vehicle running costs (fuel, pro-rata servicing for business use)
ongoing_costs.note: City-wide Nottingham radius. Estimate only — depends on vehicle type and job density per evening. Keep a mileage log; HMRC approved mileage rate (45p/mile) can be claimed if self-employed.
Covers a basic hosted site. Can be reduced to near-zero if relying solely on Google Business Profile and Facebook.

ongoing_costs.item: Marketing (paid local ads or leaflet drops)
ongoing_costs.note: Optional but recommended. Even a small Google Local Services or Facebook geo-targeted spend in Nottingham postcodes can fill evenings/weekends faster than organic alone.

startup_costs.item: Professional oven cleaning equipment (dip tank system, steam lance or caustic tank equivalent, scrapers, pads, brushes)
startup_costs.note: A portable dip tank kit with liners is the industry standard. Budget end is a basic setup; upper end buys a purpose-built heated tank. Ovenclean/Ovenu franchisees use heated dip tanks — worth pricing from a trade supplier like Oven Cleaning Supplies UK before committing.

startup_costs.item: Non-toxic cleaning chemicals (starter stock — caustic-free gels, degreasers, soak solutions)
startup_costs.note: Non-toxic/bio formulations cost more per litre than caustic trade products. Initial stock for roughly 20–30 jobs. Ongoing cost is much lower; included here only for first-purchase.

startup_costs.item: Public liability insurance (first year upfront or first few months)
startup_costs.note: Domestic cleaning trades typically pay £80–£150/year for £1–2M PLI. Some insurers (Hiscox, Protectivity, Simply Business) offer monthly. Get a quote before launch — working inside customers' homes and near electrical appliances makes this non-optional.

startup_costs.item: Vehicle costs (signage, first tax/MOT check, boot organiser/rack for equipment)
startup_costs.note: Assumes a vehicle already exists. Magnetic door signs (£30–£60) and a boot storage solution. If a vehicle needs purchasing, this line changes the entire budget; flag that separately.

startup_costs.item: Basic website / Google Business Profile setup
startup_costs.note: Google Business Profile is free and is likely your highest-ROI first move. A simple one-page site on Squarespace or Wix costs £10–£15/month; a paid designer is not needed at this stage. Domain £10–£15/year.

startup_costs.item: Printed marketing materials (leaflets for door drops in target postcodes)
startup_costs.note: Vistaprint or an equivalent. 500–1,000 A5 leaflets. Door-dropping landlord-dense streets and family areas near Nottingham city is a proven cheap acquisition channel for this type of service.
